title: ageratina esmeraldae
intro: Ageratina esmeraldae, also known as Emerald Snakeroot, is a perennial plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Ageratina esmeraldae are said to include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
